title: manulea paniculata
intro: Manulea paniculata, also known as the "Māra" or "New Zealand tea tree," is a plant species native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Manulea paniculata include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, providing relief from colds, coughs, and sore throats. Manulea paniculata is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, which is used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
